skill.name: prospero-registration-helper
skill.description: Assists researchers in generating PROSPERO registration content for meta-analyses from a title and optional protocol. Use when the user wants to draft a PROSPERO registration form.
skill.summary: Drafts PROSPERO registration content for systematic reviews and meta-analyses from a study title and optional protocol.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: The static scanner reported 56 potential issues, but all are false positives upon manual review. Markdown formatting backticks were misidentified as shell execution, and template placeholders in PROSPERO form fields were misidentified as cryptographic algorithms. This is a documentation/instruction skill with no executable code, no active network calls, and no data exfiltration risk. The only true positive is a benign GitHub URL reference in the source attribution.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URL in Documentation
low_findings.description: A hardcoded GitHub URL appears in SKILL.md as a source attribution link. This is benign documentation content and does not represent active network communication.

dangerous_patterns.title: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm (False Positive)
dangerous_patterns.description: Static scanner flagged template placeholders and markdown content as cryptographic algorithms. Files contain PROSPERO registration form templates, not cryptographic code. No cryptographic operations exist in this skill.

dangerous_patterns.title: Ruby/Shell Backtick Execution (False Positive)
dangerous_patterns.description: Static scanner misidentified Markdown formatting backticks as Ruby shell execution commands. All backtick usage is standard Markdown for inline code references and code blocks. No shell execution occurs.

dangerous_patterns.title: System Reconnaissance (False Positive)
dangerous_patterns.description: Static scanner flagged a quality guideline about output discipline as system reconnaissance. Line 56 of SKILL.md states: 'keep results reproducible, identify assumptions explicitly, and avoid undocumented side effects.' This is a code quality instruction, not a reconnaissance attempt.

content.user_title: Draft a PROSPERO Registration for Your Systematic Review
content.value_statement: Researchers often struggle to complete the detailed PROSPERO registration form for systematic reviews and meta-analyses. This skill generates the registration content from a study title and optional protocol, following the official PROSPERO structure.
actual_capabilities: Extract PICO elements (Population, Intervention, Comparison, Outcome) from a study title
Generate PROSPERO registration sections including basic details, eligibility criteria, timeline, and methods
Calculate study timeline dates with start date and anticipated 28-day completion date
Design PubMed search strategies based on PICO framework
Format output to match the official PROSPERO registration form structure
Preserve PROSPERO system dropdown options while filling in analysis-based content
limitations: Cannot submit the registration to the PROSPERO website directly - manual submission is required
Requires a clear study title for accurate PICO extraction; vague titles reduce output quality
Does not perform actual literature searches or extract data from published studies
Generated content requires researcher review and verification before submission

use_cases.title: First-time PROSPERO Registration
use_cases.description: A researcher preparing their first systematic review needs help completing the PROSPERO registration form correctly with proper structure and terminology.

use_cases.title: Protocol-driven Registration
use_cases.description: A researcher has a written protocol and needs to translate it into the structured PROSPERO format, extracting key elements from the protocol text.

use_cases.title: Rapid Registration Draft
use_cases.description: A team lead needs a quick draft of the registration form to share with collaborators for review and feedback before final submission.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Review Title Registration
prompt_templates.prompt: I need to register a meta-analysis on PROSPERO. The title is: [paste your review title here]. Please generate the basic details section of the PROSPERO registration form.
prompt_templates.scenario: First-time user with only a study title

prompt_templates.title: Full Registration with Protocol
prompt_templates.prompt: I have a protocol for my systematic review. Title: [title]. Protocol: [paste protocol text]. Please generate the complete PROSPERO registration form including all sections.
prompt_templates.scenario: User has a complete protocol document

prompt_templates.title: PICO Extraction and Search Strategy
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me design a search strategy for my PROSPERO registration. My review is about: [describe review topic]. Extract PICO elements and create a PubMed search strategy.
prompt_templates.scenario: User needs help with search methodology

prompt_templates.title: Eligibility Criteria Refinement
prompt_templates.prompt: I am working on the eligibility criteria section of my PROSPERO registration. The review covers: [population, intervention, details]. Generate detailed inclusion and exclusion criteria using future tense.

output_examples.input: I need to register a systematic review titled 'Efficacy of Cognitive Behavioral Therapy for Insomnia in Adults'
output: 1.*Review title. Efficacy of Cognitive Behavioral Therapy for Insomnia in Adults

2.*Condition or domain being studied. Insomnia disorder in adult populations. Cognitive Behavioral Therapy for Insomnia (CBT-I) is a non-pharmacological intervention that addresses sleep-related behaviors and thoughts.
3.*Keywords. Cognitive Behavioral Therapy, Insomnia, Adults, Sleep Quality, Randomized Controlled Trials

output_examples.input: I need help with the eligibility criteria for my review on Mediterranean diet and cardiovascular outcomes in elderly patients
output: 1.*population. Inclusion: Adults aged 65 years and older. Exclusion: Patients with pre-existing cardiovascular disease, participants in institutional care.
2.*Intervention(s), exposure(s). Mediterranean diet intervention defined by a Mediterranean Diet Score or similar validated tool.
3.*Comparator(s)/control(s). Usual diet or minimal dietary intervention.

best_practices: Provide a clear and complete study title before generating the registration form for best results.
Review all generated content carefully and verify PICO extraction accuracy before submission.
Include your protocol text when available for more accurate and detailed registration content.
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill to generate clinical recommendations or patient-specific medical advice.
Do not submit generated content without verifying that placeholder options inside curly braces are correctly selected.
Do not use this skill for registered reports or protocols that do not follow the PROSPERO format.

faq.question: What is PROSPERO?
faq.answer: PROSPERO is an international database of registered systematic reviews. Researchers register their review protocols to promote transparency and reduce duplication.

faq.question: Does this skill submit my registration to PROSPERO?
faq.answer: No. This skill generates the content for the registration form. You must submit it yourself through the PROSPERO website.

faq.question: What information do I need to provide?
faq.answer: You need at least a study title. A full protocol document will produce more accurate and complete results.

faq.question: How are the timeline dates calculated?
faq.answer: The start date is set to today. The anticipated completion date is calculated as 28 days from today.

faq.question: Can I use this for any type of review?
faq.answer: This skill is designed for systematic reviews and meta-analyses following the standard PROSPERO form structure.

faq.question: Will the generated content pass PROSPERO review?
faq.answer: The content follows the PROSPERO form structure, but you should always review and verify all information before submission.
